rendering_strategy.rationale: This platform has three distinct content tiers requiring different rendering strategies: (1) Static editorial content (onsen guides, etiquette articles, regional spotlights) should be SSG for maximum performance and SEO — these pages change infrequently and benefit from CDN edge caching. (2) Ryokan listing pages should use ISR with 60-second revalidation — property details change infrequently but availability/pricing may update, and ISR provides the SEO benefits of static generation with near-real-time data. (3) Search results, booking flow, user dashboard, and community features require SSR for personalized, real-time content. Next.js App Router supports all three strategies per-route, making this hybrid approach natural.

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Unnecessary server load for static content pages (guides, about, etiquette). Would increase Vercel serverless function invocations and cost without benefit for content that changes weekly at most.
Cannot support real-time search results, personalized recommendations, or dynamic availability/pricing. Build times would become prohibitive as ryokan inventory grows beyond 1,000 properties.

SEO is critical for a content-driven discovery platform targeting international travelers via organic search. Client-side rendering would severely impact discoverability for queries like 'best onsen ryokan Hakone' which are the primary user acquisition channel.

route_strategy_map./: SSG — homepage with curated collections, regenerated daily
route_strategy_map./ryokan/[slug]: ISR (revalidate: 60) — property detail pages with availability
route_strategy_map./search: SSR — dynamic search with filters, personalized results
route_strategy_map./onsen-guide/[slug]: SSG — editorial content, rebuilt on publish

packages.name: next
packages.purpose: Core framework — App Router, server components, API routes, ISR, image optimization

packages.name: next-intl
packages.purpose: Internationalization — JP/EN/CN-S/CN-T/KR locale routing, message formatting, date/currency localization. Integrates natively with App Router and server components.

packages.name: next-auth
packages.purpose: Authentication — social login (Google, LINE, WeChat), email/password, session management. LINE login is critical for Japanese users; WeChat for Chinese tourists.

packages.name: @supabase/supabase-js
packages.purpose: Database client — Postgres via Supabase for ryokan listings, user profiles, bookings, reviews, diary entries. Supabase provides Row Level Security, real-time subscriptions for availability updates, and built-in storage for images.

packages.name: stripe
packages.purpose: Payment processing — multi-currency support (JPY, USD, EUR, CNY, KRW), Stripe Connect for marketplace payouts to ryokan, JCT invoice compliance, 3D Secure for international cards.

packages.name: zod
packages.purpose: Schema validation — type-safe validation for booking forms, search parameters, API request/response bodies. Shared schemas between client and server.

packages.name: @tanstack/react-query
packages.purpose: Server state management — caching and synchronization for search results, availability checks, user bookings. Provides optimistic updates for booking flow and background refetching for availability.

packages.name: react-datepicker
packages.purpose: Date range selection — check-in/check-out date picker for booking flow. Supports locale-aware date formatting (Japanese calendar conventions).

packages.name: embla-carousel-react
packages.purpose: Image carousel — ryokan photo galleries, onsen bath image sliders. Lightweight, touch-friendly, accessible. Used on property detail pages and search result cards.

packages.name: react-map-gl
packages.purpose: Interactive maps — ryokan location display, regional onsen area maps, nearby attractions. Wraps Mapbox GL JS with React components. Used on search results and property detail pages.

packages.name: lucide-react
packages.purpose: Icon library — consistent iconography across UI (search, filters, bath types, amenities, navigation). Tree-shakeable for minimal bundle impact.

packages.name: react-hot-toast
packages.purpose: Toast notifications — booking confirmations, error messages, availability alerts. Lightweight and accessible.

external_apis.name: Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage)
external_apis.purpose: Primary database, file storage for ryokan images, and optional auth provider
external_apis.rate_limits: Free tier: 500MB database, 1GB storage, 50K monthly active users. Pro tier ($25/mo): 8GB database, 100GB storage, unlimited MAU. No hard API rate limits on Pro tier but connection pooling recommended.
external_apis.cost_implications: Pro tier at $25/month covers MVP. Storage costs scale at $0.021/GB beyond 100GB. Database compute scales with usage. Estimated $25-100/month for Year 1.
external_apis.fallback: Can migrate to Neon Postgres + Cloudflare R2 storage if Supabase pricing becomes prohibitive at scale.

external_apis.name: Stripe Payments API
external_apis.purpose: Payment processing, Stripe Connect for marketplace payouts to ryokan owners
external_apis.rate_limits: 100 read requests/second, 100 write requests/second in live mode. No monthly caps.
external_apis.cost_implications: Japan: 3.6% per transaction. International cards: 3.6% + currency conversion fee. Stripe Connect: additional 0.25% + ¥10 per payout. For ¥200M Year 1 GMV: ~¥7.2M in Stripe fees (~$48K).
external_apis.fallback: PAY.JP or GMO Payment Gateway as Japan-specific alternatives with potentially lower domestic rates (3.0-3.25%).

external_apis.name: Mapbox GL JS
external_apis.purpose: Interactive maps for ryokan locations, regional exploration, and nearby onsen discovery
external_apis.rate_limits: Free tier: 50,000 map loads/month, 100,000 geocoding requests/month. Pay-as-you-go beyond that.
external_apis.cost_implications: Free tier likely sufficient for MVP. Beyond 50K loads: $5 per 1,000 map loads. Estimated $0-50/month Year 1.
external_apis.fallback: Leaflet.js with OpenStreetMap tiles (fully free) if map costs become significant. react-leaflet package available.

external_apis.name: Google Places API (Autocomplete + Details)
external_apis.purpose: Location autocomplete for search (e.g., 'Hakone', 'Kusatsu'), fetching place details and coordinates
external_apis.rate_limits: Autocomplete: $2.83 per 1,000 requests. Place Details: $17 per 1,000 requests.
external_apis.cost_implications: With aggressive client-side debouncing (300ms) and session tokens, estimated $50-200/month for Year 1 traffic levels.
external_apis.fallback: Mapbox Geocoding API (included in free tier up to 100K/month) or self-hosted search with pre-indexed onsen region database.

external_apis.name: Vercel Image Optimization
external_apis.purpose: On-the-fly image resizing and format conversion (WebP/AVIF) for ryokan photos
external_apis.rate_limits: Pro plan: 5,000 source images optimized/month included. $5 per additional 1,000.
external_apis.cost_implications: With ~500 ryokan at ~20 images each = 10,000 source images. Estimated $25-50/month beyond Pro plan inclusion.
external_apis.fallback: Supabase Storage transformations or Cloudinary free tier (25K transformations/month).

external_apis.name: LINE Login API
external_apis.purpose: Social authentication for Japanese users via LINE (dominant messaging app in Japan)
external_apis.rate_limits: No published rate limits for authentication. LINE Messaging API: 500 free messages/month.
external_apis.cost_implications: Free for authentication. LINE Official Account for notifications: free tier up to 500 messages/month, then ¥5,000/month for 5,000 messages.
external_apis.fallback: Email/password auth as primary, with LINE as optional social login.

serverless_function_timeout.implications: Booking confirmation flow involves Stripe payment + database write + email notification. Must be completed within 60 seconds. Recommend splitting into: (1) Stripe payment intent creation (fast), (2) webhook-based confirmation (async, no timeout concern).
serverless_function_timeout.mitigation: Use Stripe webhooks for post-payment processing instead of synchronous API routes. Background functions (up to 300 seconds on Pro) available for heavy operations like batch email sends or report generation.

bundle_size.implications: react-map-gl + Mapbox GL JS is ~200KB gzipped — significant but within limits. next-intl with 5 locale message files adds ~50-100KB per locale. Total bundle should stay well under 50MB.
bundle_size.mitigation: Use dynamic imports for map component (only loaded on pages that display maps). Lazy-load image carousel. Split locale messages by route namespace to avoid loading all translations upfront.

edge_functions.recommendation: Use Edge Runtime for locale detection middleware (Accept-Language header parsing + geo-IP) to route users to appropriate language version. Keep data-heavy operations in Node.js serverless functions.
edge_functions.implications: Edge functions have limited Node.js API access — cannot use fs, crypto (full), or most native modules. Supabase client works in Edge. Stripe SDK requires Node.js runtime.

cold_starts.implications: Serverless cold starts on Vercel Pro: typically 200-500ms. Acceptable for most routes but may cause slight delay on first search request after idle period.
cold_starts.mitigation: Use ISR for frequently accessed pages (ryokan listings) to serve from CDN cache and avoid serverless invocation entirely. Consider Vercel's Fluid Compute for high-traffic search endpoints.

bandwidth.implications: Image-heavy platform — each ryokan page may serve 2-5MB of photos. With Vercel Image Optimization and next/image, images are served in WebP/AVIF at appropriate sizes, reducing bandwidth 50-70%. 1TB should cover ~200K-500K page views/month.

browser_apis.purpose: Enable 'onsen near me' search for users currently in Japan. Show nearby ryokan on map.
browser_apis.fallback: IP-based geolocation via Vercel Edge middleware (X-Vercel-IP-Country, X-Vercel-IP-City headers). Manual location selection with search autocomplete. Default to Tokyo region for Japanese IP addresses.

browser_apis.purpose: Lazy loading of ryokan images in search results and gallery views. Infinite scroll for search results and community diary feed.
browser_apis.fallback: Supported in all modern browsers (97%+ coverage). For older browsers, use native loading='lazy' attribute on img elements as basic fallback.

browser_apis.purpose: Native share dialog for sharing ryokan listings and onsen diary entries on mobile devices.
browser_apis.fallback: Custom share modal with copy-to-clipboard and direct links to Twitter/LINE/WeChat. navigator.share availability check before showing native share button.

browser_apis.purpose: Offline access to saved/favorited ryokan listings and onsen guides for travelers with spotty connectivity in rural onsen areas.
browser_apis.fallback: Progressive enhancement — offline features only available for users who have visited pages while online. next-pwa or serwist package for Next.js service worker integration. Without service worker, standard browser cache provides limited offline support.

browser_apis.purpose: Locale-aware date formatting (Japanese era calendar option), currency formatting (¥25,000 vs $167 vs ₩225,000), and number formatting across 5 supported locales.
browser_apis.fallback: Universally supported in modern browsers. Polyfill via @formatjs/intl-datetimeformat for edge cases in older Android WebViews.
